channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The latest DJI Osmo Action 6 image leak gives us our first look at the camera actually in action
item.description: Another picture of the DJI Osmo Action 6 has emerged, and it's likely that more leaks are going to follow.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: The truth about finding cheap airfare, from the head of Google Flights
item.description: Google Flights is one of the most popular flight aggregators on the web. The site lets users search millions of flights to find the best routes and prices that meet their needs. Unsurprisingly, millions of people use Google Flights to find the best deals on holiday tickets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What Hollywood’s next potential merger means for streaming
item.description: One of Hollywood’s crown jewels is on the block: Warner Bros. Discovery, the parent company of HBO, CNN, and major movie franchises like Harry Potter and the DC Universe, officially confirmed this week that it is open to a sale.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Here are 20-plus kid tools for higher-quality screen time
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Not everything creative needs a prompt. The web is increasingly flooded with AI-generated images and videos, much of it aimed at kids.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Her work brings clean water and new opportunities to tribal communities across the country
item.description: "When we remove some of those barriers to access to safe drinking water, clean drinking water, that we're really freeing up people's time," said Melissa de Vera The post Her work brings clean water and new opportunities to tribal communities across the country first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Sanders: Government should break up OpenAI
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said he believes the government should break up OpenAI, underscoring his concerns about AI's potential impact on the job market and interpersonal relationships.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Sora is even fooling human deepfake detectors
item.description: It used to be that artificial intelligence would leave behind helpful clues that an image it produced was not, in fact, real. Previous generations of the technology might give a person an extra finger or even an additional limb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration pushes to get AI data centers on the grid faster
item.description: The Trump administration issued a proposal late Thursday that aims to help AI data centers get connected to the electric grid more quickly.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: The hot issues for CIOs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025
item.description: The TechCrunch Disrupt conference has long provided valuable insights to and about startups and their investors — but CIOs can learn by talking with startups too, and there’s plenty to learn about at this year’s event.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Nvidia just invested in a battery recycling company
item.description: As Nvidia’s value has soared—becoming the first public company to hit $4 trillion in market capitalization earlier this year—it’s been pouring money into AI startups. Its venture arm, NVentures, is also backing less expected bets.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Nissan adds solar roof to Japan's most popular little EV – and it promises to add 1,800 miles of free range a year
item.description: An extendable solar roof can add useable mileage to Nissan's diminutive EV and the tech is going into production.
item.dc:creator: Leon Poultney, TechRadar

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: Elon Musk literally left a government job with a black eye, has seen profits at his car company sink and must worry about federal regulators disrupting his plans to unleash a fleet of driverless taxis on America's roads in the next few years.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Delete the fake VPN app stealing Android users' money
item.description: Cleafy researchers discover fake VPN streaming app Mobdro Pro that installs Klopatra banking Trojan, giving attackers full control over Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Serious vulnerability found in Rust library
item.description: Developers creating projects in the Rust programming language, as well as IT leaders with Rust-based applications in their environments, should pay attention to a serious vulnerability found in one of the programming language’s libraries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI chatbots are wrong about news 45% of the time, study finds
item.description: A new international study coordinated by the European Broadcasting Union (EBU) and led by the BBC shows that AI assistants distort news content nearly half of the time. The research included 22 public service broadcasters in 18 countries and 14 languages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s top strategic technology trends for 2026
item.description: To no one’s surprise, AI took center stage at the Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo going on this week in Orlando, Fla., where the research firm made its annual presentation of the core technology trends set to shape enterprise IT in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China on Sunday accused the U.S. National Security Agency of carrying out cyberattacks on its national time center, saying any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
